La Liga chief claims Fifa is ‘destroying football industry’ and calls for Infantino to quit

• Javier Tebas: ‘Fifa are making irresponsible decisions’

  • ‘Hydration breaks are a lie … it was a break for advertising’

La Liga’s president, Javier Tebas, has accused Fifa of “destroying the football industry” and called for its president, Gianni Infantino, to quit. Tebas spoke out as the dust settled on the 2026 World Cup with suggestions that the 2030 tournament could be increased from 48 to 64 teams.

“Increasing the number of national teams makes no sense,” Tebas said in an interview with the Italian newspaper La Gazzetta dello Sport. “The football industry is not just the World Cup, which is the most important event. Not everything can revolve around the World Cup. It is national competitions that support this sport.
